title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: We are going to start with this. This is called tapoki.
I'm going to say six, only because I guess I'm more of a spicy eater?
Korean food, we're going to eat rice and that's it.

title: will / shall
examples: He will be trying out different kinds of Korean food that we got from a market here, but actually he's already been to Korea.
Sometimes they'll have fish cakes inside or I like it with ramen noodles.
So they'll do different toppings and they'll also do different kinds of sauces.
This is very popular like when people go camping, they'll have this in the morning for breakfast after drinking a lot of beers or soju the night before.
So if you're eating noodles or rice, you'll eat it together.

title: will / won't
examples: Most people won't eat this just plain.

title: will be doing / will have done
examples: He will be trying out different kinds of Korean food that we got from a market here, but actually he's already been to Korea.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: Let me see if I can remember how to do this.
So if you can take a small piece of the kimchi, and then if you can get some noodles to eat it together with this, so kimchi is usually served as a side dish.
I can see why you'd eat it together.
Wow, I can see where I get it from.
I could make a meal out of this.

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: I might say. We've got the whole kitchen smelling good.
You may have another one.
I think it might do go all right.

title: have to / must
examples: Do you think the shin ramen or the chapegetti if you had to choose?
If I had to choose, I'd say the shin ramen.
I'd have to have some of this with it.
I would have to have something with that.

title: should (advice)
examples: Do you think I should open a tapoki shop or bunga pung stand in Saginaw?

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: If you were to just look at this, what would you guess that it is?
I would guess it's some type of noodles, cheese, and sauce.
So out of ten, what would you rate this?
I don't know if I'd try it for breakfast.
I'd go about eight on it.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: If you were to just look at this, what would you guess that it is?
Do you think the shin ramen or the chapegetti if you had to choose?
If I had to choose, I'd say the shin ramen.
Um, I'd give it a ten if I could mix it with this or that.
So out of everything, I know it's hard, but if you could only choose one food, what would you say was your favorite?

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: We are going to start with this. This is called tapoki.
So this is called hit bun.

title: have something done
examples: I might say. We've got the whole kitchen smelling good.
